title: AIRED training-set build — decision log
what: The engineering and data-curation decisions made while turning this repo's history into instruction→implementation training pairs (aired_pairs.jsonl).

dataset.format: JSONL, one {"messages":[{user},{assistant}]} per line; user=instruction, assistant=unified diff

decisions.decision: Build pairs from merged PRs (title+body→diff) and substantive commits (message→diff); closed issues were in scope but the repo had none (0).
decisions.rationale: PRs/commits carry an explicit human-written intent paired with a real implementation — the cleanest instruction→code signal available.

decisions.decision: Compute diffs from local git (full content) and pull PR text from the GitHub API; use two-dot base..head for a PR's own commits.
decisions.rationale: Local git gives complete, authoritative diffs without API truncation.
decisions.alternative_rejected: Three-dot A...B for the commit list — it leaked base-side commits into PR membership (fixed; see BUG-1).

decisions.decision: Skip brand/logo/image-asset PRs and commits entirely (e.g. PR #22, the Facebook/logo assets).
decisions.rationale: The goal is engineering work (type guards, Volley logic, HLS, player, layout), not design assets.

decisions.decision: Skip the Phase-0 scaffold and copy-only commits (README, Covenant prose).
decisions.rationale: Generated scaffolding and prose edits aren't engineering instruction→code signal.

decisions.decision: Strip lockfiles and generated files from every diff before pairing.
decisions.rationale: Generated churn (e.g. a 1,700-line package-lock.json) drowns the real change and teaches noise.
decisions.result: Lockfile content never appears in the corpus.

decisions.decision: Keep filename references to generated files (e.g. a Dockerfile `COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./`).
decisions.rationale: That's a real, meaningful engineering line — a reference, not generated content.

decisions.decision: Adopt a ~150-line net-diff ceiling; changes under it become one whole-unit pair.
decisions.rationale: Small, self-contained diffs are higher-quality supervised targets than sprawling multi-file features.

decisions.decision: For PRs over the ceiling, decompose into member commits only when every member also fits; otherwise treat as irreducible and drop+list.
decisions.rationale: Honors a strict reading of the ceiling and avoids emitting monster diffs.

decisions.decision: Lenient-salvage small member commits trapped inside dropped PRs (e.g. 8aec335, a 21-line worker fix).
decisions.rationale: Recovers genuinely self-contained small changes that the strict rule would discard.

decisions.decision: Split remaining large changes per file; keep a single coherent file/hunk whole up to 400 lines (the coherent-change exception); drop single files/hunks over ~400 lines and list them.
decisions.rationale: A coherent change shouldn't be chopped mid-logic, but a 400+ line single file can't be a clean target.

decisions.decision: When hunk-splitting, pack adjacent hunks of a file up to the 150-line ceiling.
decisions.rationale: Avoids over-fragmenting into tiny, context-poor pairs.

decisions.decision: Each per-file pair gets an instruction SCOPED to that file's actual change, grounded in its real symbols/behavior — never the shared full PR body.
decisions.rationale: Pairing 20 files with one identical feature-spec teaches the model that a broad multi-part request maps to one arbitrary file. The instruction must match the diff in front of it.
decisions.result: 184 file-scoped instructions, each authored from its own diff.

decisions.decision: Drop a fragment that only makes sense as part of the whole feature rather than pair it with a coherent-sounding but mislabeled instruction.
decisions.rationale: A wrong label is worse than a missing example.

decisions.decision: Pre-drop modified-file wiring (no self-contained symbol); author the rest by reviewing each file's diff individually.
decisions.rationale: Integration/mounting edits can't be described standalone; substantive file changes can.

decisions.decision: Integrity gate: every kept instruction must name its own file.
decisions.rationale: Cheap, strong check that each instruction is aligned to its diff.

decisions.decision: Run a mandatory, line-oriented secret scan on every emitted diff; redact hits in place.
decisions.rationale: Training data must never carry credentials; line-oriented scanning can't span diff lines or corrupt a patch (see BUG-2).

decisions.decision: Harvest the repo's real R2 account-id literal and redact it everywhere it appears.
decisions.rationale: It was hardcoded as a config.js default and documented in the worker README, not just the env example — pattern-anchored matching alone would miss those.
decisions.result: Redacted in env, README, and source default.

decisions.decision: Redact the public publishable anon key for cleanliness; keep the public project-ref hostname.
decisions.rationale: The anon key is key-shaped; the hostname is just the public API endpoint and is harmless.

decisions.decision: Decode HTML entities and strip commit/PR footers and bare claude.ai/code session URLs from instructions.
decisions.rationale: Those are trailers, not part of the task spec.

decisions.decision: Never ingest the dataset's own output files, and end the commit range at the last engineering commit.
decisions.rationale: Prevents the build from feeding on its own prior output (see BUG-3).

decisions.decision: Deliver pure {"messages":[…]} JSONL (upload-ready) and keep all provenance in a separate meta.json.
decisions.rationale: A leading # header breaks strict JSONL parsers; provenance belongs beside the data, not inside it.

bugs_caught_and_fixed.issue: PR member-commit lists were computed with three-dot `git log A...B` (symmetric), polluting them with base-side commits.
bugs_caught_and_fixed.impact: Wrong decomposition membership and risk of mis-attributing standalone commits.
bugs_caught_and_fixed.fix: Switched to two-dot `A..B` (a PR's own commits).

bugs_caught_and_fixed.issue: A secret regex used `\s*`, which spanned a newline on an empty `R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=` and swallowed the next diff line as the 'value'.
bugs_caught_and_fixed.impact: False positive AND a corrupted patch (a +line replaced by a bare marker).
bugs_caught_and_fixed.fix: Rewrote the scanner to be line-oriented — it strips the diff prefix, scans content only, and can never cross diff lines.

bugs_caught_and_fixed.issue: After committing the deliverables, the build saw its own output files as a source commit.
bugs_caught_and_fixed.fix: Excluded the deliverable files and ended the commit range at the last engineering commit.

bugs_caught_and_fixed.issue: Per-file pairs initially shared the entire PR/commit body as their instruction.
bugs_caught_and_fixed.impact: Systematic mislabeling — many files mapped to one broad spec.
bugs_caught_and_fixed.fix: Rescoped every split pair to a per-file instruction grounded in that file's diff; dropped fragments that couldn't stand alone.
